title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat as it exploits compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code. This could lead to widespread data encryption, service disruptions, financial losses, and reputational damage for organizations relying on cloud environments.
recommended_actions: Implement strict security controls and monitoring for CI/CD pipelines, including access restrictions, code integrity checks, and regular audits.
Ensure cloud environments are patched and up-to-date, and deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to identify and mitigate ransomware activity.
Conduct regular backups of critical data and store them in isolated, secure environments to enable recovery in case of an attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: Network-AI before 5.13.4 contains an improper cryptographic signature verification vulnerability...
description: Network-AI before 5.13.4 contains an improper cryptographic signature verification vulnerability in APSAdapter where the default local verifier accepts any non-empty string as valid. Unauthenticated attackers can submit forged APS delegation payloads with arbitrary scopes to bypass signature verification and obtain signed permission-grant tokens for sensitive resources including SHELL_EXEC.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass cryptographic signature verification and obtain signed permission-grant tokens. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive resources, including the ability to execute arbitrary shell commands (SHELL_EXEC), resulting in potential data breaches, system compromise, and operational disruptions.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Network-AI to version 5.13.4 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Audit all systems and logs for signs of unauthorized access or exploitation of the vulnerability.
Implement additional monitoring and alerting for suspicious activities, particularly around APS delegation payloads and SHELL_EXEC operations.

title: FreeRDP before 3.28.0 (affected 3.x through 3.27.1) contains a double-free vulnerability in...
description: FreeRDP before 3.28.0 (affected 3.x through 3.27.1) contains a double-free vulnerability in freerdp_client_rdp_file_apply_to_settings() (client/common/file.c) when parsing the selectedmonitors field of a .rdp connection file. The MonitorIds array is allocated through the settings object, and a raw non-owning pointer to it is freed on the strtoul error path without clearing settings->MonitorIds, leaving it dangling; at teardown freerdp_settings_free() frees the same buffer again. An attacker who

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64621.

title: SurrealDB before v2.6.1 (and before v3.0.0-beta.3) contains a denial of service vulnerability in...
description: SurrealDB before v2.6.1 (and before v3.0.0-beta.3) contains a denial of service vulnerability in its embedded JavaScript scripting engine, which is enabled via the --allow-scripting capability (disabled by default). Any user able to execute arbitrary queries — including unauthenticated guests when --allow-guests is enabled — can use built-in string functions to construct a large string and pass it to the JavaScript runtime for compilation, triggering a null pointer dereference in the underlying

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-63762.

title: Network-AI (npm: network-ai) versions 5.12.2 through 5.13.3 fail to apply the configured...
description: Network-AI (npm: network-ai) versions 5.12.2 through 5.13.3 fail to apply the configured authorization check (checkAuth/secret) to the ApprovalInbox GET read routes, so even when an operator configures a secret, unauthenticated actors can access sensitive approval request details. The GET /approvals/?status=all, GET /approvals/:id, GET /approvals/stats, and GET /approvals/sse routes disclose full ApprovalEntry content including action/target shell-command strings, file paths, justifications, and

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated actors to access sensitive approval request details, including shell commands, file paths, and justifications. This can lead to unauthorized data exposure, privilege escalation, and potential exploitation of sensitive operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to a patched version of Network-AI (npm: network-ai) beyond 5.13.3 to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement additional network-layer access controls (e.g., IP whitelisting, VPN) to restrict access to the affected endpoints.
Conduct a security audit to identify and remediate any unauthorized access or data exposure resulting from this vulnerability.

title: FreeRDP before 3.28.0 (affected <=3.27.1) contains a heap-based buffer overflow in...
description: FreeRDP before 3.28.0 (affected <=3.27.1) contains a heap-based buffer overflow in crypto_rsa_common() (libfreerdp/crypto/crypto.c). The function writes the modular-exponentiation result into the caller's output buffer via BN_bn2bin() and only afterward checks output_length > out_length, so out-of-bounds bytes are written before the bounds check. On the server side, when a client selects RDP Standard Security, the encrypted client random is decrypted into a fixed 32-byte buffer. Because the serv

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64620.

title: SurrealDB before 2.5.0 and before 3.0.0-beta.3 contains a confused deputy privilege escalation...
description: SurrealDB before 2.5.0 and before 3.0.0-beta.3 contains a confused deputy privilege escalation vulnerability. Unprivileged users (e.g., those with the database editor role) can create or modify fields containing futures, functions, or closures. Because these are executed in the context of the invoking/querying user rather than their creator, an attacker can plant malicious logic that executes with a higher-privileged user's permissions when that user reads or writes the affected record. This can

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unprivileged users to escalate their privileges by injecting malicious logic into database fields, which can execute with higher-privileged user permissions. This could lead to unauthorized data access, manipulation, or potential compromise of sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Upgrade SurrealDB to version 2.5.0 or 3.0.0-beta.3 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Audit database roles and permissions to ensure unprivileged users have limited access to sensitive operations.
Implement monitoring and logging to detect unusual database activity, such as unauthorized field modifications.

title: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 fails to enforce recursion depth limits in the type/kind parser when...
description: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 fails to enforce recursion depth limits in the type/kind parser when processing nested type annotations. Authenticated attackers can send queries with deeply nested type annotations to exhaust server memory and crash the process.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-63759.

title: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 fails to enforce the configured recursion depth limit in the value and...
description: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 fails to enforce the configured recursion depth limit in the value and JSON parser when processing nested braces, brackets, or parentheses. Unauthenticated attackers can send deeply nested JSON payloads to the WebSocket /rpc endpoint to exhaust server memory and crash the process.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-63760.

title: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 silently substitutes the ES384 algorithm when a JWT access method is...
description: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 silently substitutes the ES384 algorithm when a JWT access method is configured with ALGORITHM ES512 (DEFINE ACCESS ... TYPE JWT ALGORITHM ES512), because the underlying jsonwebtoken crate (v10.x) has no ES512 variant and the mapping defaults to ES384 without any error, warning, or log message. Users who supply the correct P-521 key for ES512 experience authentication handshake failures due to the curve mismatch with ES384 (which expects P-384), and tokens are rejected by

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-63761.

title: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain a session hijacking vulnerability where the HTTP /rpc...
description: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain a session hijacking vulnerability where the HTTP /rpc sessions method returns attached session UUIDs without authentication and accepts arbitrary session fields with no ownership verification. Unauthenticated attackers can enumerate session UUIDs and impersonate authenticated sessions to read, write, delete data and escalate privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to hijack sessions, impersonate users, and perform unauthorized actions such as reading, writing, deleting data, and escalating privileges. This could lead to data breaches, service disruptions, and unauthorized access to sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to SurrealDB version 3.1.0 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Review and monitor server logs for suspicious activity, particularly unauthorized session enumeration or access attempts.
Implement additional security controls such as stricter authentication mechanisms and session validation to mitigate potential exploitation.

title: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 evaluates user-supplied WHERE clauses in SELECT statements (and SET/MERGE...
description: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 evaluates user-supplied WHERE clauses in SELECT statements (and SET/MERGE/CONTENT/PATCH clauses in UPDATE, UPSERT, INSERT ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE, and RELATE update-variant statements) against full record data before enforcing PERMISSIONS FOR SELECT WHERE restrictions. An authenticated user — including Record and Scope users — can exploit this ordering flaw to read the full contents of any table in the database they are authenticated against, bypassing table-level permissi

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-63755.

title: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 fails to refresh authentication state in LIVE SELECT subscriptions when...
description: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 fails to refresh authentication state in LIVE SELECT subscriptions when session state changes. Attackers can continue receiving real-time notifications under revoked or expired session credentials until the connection closes.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-63753.

title: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain a denial of service vulnerability where malicious LIVE...
description: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain a denial of service vulnerability where malicious LIVE queries with WHERE clauses that evaluate to errors cause all CREATE, UPDATE, and DELETE operations on the watched table to fail. An authenticated user with only select permission can prevent write operations on a table for any user, including root, by registering a LIVE query that triggers evaluation errors until the query is killed or the session ends.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows an authenticated user with minimal permissions to disrupt critical database operations, including CREATE, UPDATE, and DELETE actions, by exploiting a flaw in LIVE queries. This can lead to significant downtime, data processing delays, and potential business disruptions.
recommended_actions: Upgrade SurrealDB to version 3.1.0 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict access to LIVE queries and ensure only trusted users have permissions to execute them.
Monitor database logs for unusual LIVE query activity and terminate malicious sessions promptly.

title: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain an authorization bypass vulnerability in the KILL...
description: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain an authorization bypass vulnerability in the KILL statement that allows authenticated database users to terminate other users' LIVE SELECT subscriptions. Attackers can issue KILL statements with target live query UUIDs to disrupt real-time data subscriptions of other users without ownership verification.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-63758.

title: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability in the RELATE statement...
description: SurrealDB before 3.1.0 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability in the RELATE statement that allows authenticated users with CREATE permission to overwrite existing edge records without UPDATE permission. Attackers can issue a RELATE statement with a SET id clause pointing to an existing edge id, causing the storage layer to silently overwrite the target record instead of rejecting the operation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-63752.

title: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain a time-of-check/time-of-use race condition in the HTTP ...
description: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain a time-of-check/time-of-use race condition in the HTTP /rpc endpoint that allows unauthenticated requests to inherit authenticated session state. Unauthenticated attackers can send concurrent requests to the /rpc endpoint while legitimate authenticated traffic is active to execute operations with hijacked user privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-63756.

title: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain a field-level permission bypass vulnerability in JSON...
description: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain a field-level permission bypass vulnerability in JSON Patch operations that allows authenticated users to read protected fields. Attackers can use UPDATE PATCH with an empty from pointer in copy or move operations to duplicate all record fields, including those restricted by field-level SELECT permissions, into attacker-chosen destination fields.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users to bypass field-level permissions in SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0, potentially exposing sensitive data that should remain protected. This could lead to unauthorized data access, data leaks, or compliance violations.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to SurrealDB version 3.1.0 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Review and audit user access permissions to ensure that only authorized users have access to sensitive data.
Monitor database logs for unusual UPDATE PATCH operations and implement additional logging to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain an authentication bypass vulnerability in LIVE SELECT...
description: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain an authentication bypass vulnerability in LIVE SELECT subscriptions where permission expressions referencing $value, $before, $after, or $event are evaluated against attacker-controlled bindings instead of actual documents. Authenticated subscribers can bind chosen values to these parameter names and register LIVE SELECT queries to receive notifications for records that SELECT permission expressions should have hidden.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers to bypass permission checks in LIVE SELECT queries, potentially exposing sensitive data that should be restricted. This could lead to unauthorized access to confidential information and data leakage.
recommended_actions: Upgrade SurrealDB to version 3.1.0 or later to patch the authentication bypass vulnerability.
Review and audit LIVE SELECT queries and permissions to ensure sensitive data is properly restricted.
Monitor database activity for unusual patterns or unauthorized access attempts, especially from authenticated users.

title: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain an information disclosure vulnerability where...
description: SurrealDB versions before 3.1.0 contain an information disclosure vulnerability where authenticated users with UPDATE access can read field values hidden by field-level SELECT permissions through error messages. Attackers can trigger arithmetic or extend operations on hidden fields to embed raw operand values in error responses, bypassing field-level access controls.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-63748.

title: SurrealDB before 3.1.5 contains a server-side request forgery vulnerability in the JWKS fetcher...
description: SurrealDB before 3.1.5 contains a server-side request forgery vulnerability in the JWKS fetcher that follows HTTP redirects without re-validating redirect targets against network capabilities. Attackers with Owner role can configure a JWKS URL pointing to an allowlisted host that redirects to blocked internal addresses, bypassing network access controls.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers with Owner role to exploit server-side request forgery (SSRF) by bypassing network access controls, potentially exposing sensitive internal systems or data to unauthorized access.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to SurrealDB version 3.1.5 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict the Owner role to trusted users and regularly audit role assignments.
Implement strict network access controls and validate redirect targets in JWKS fetcher configurations.

title: CVE-2026-15525 - A vulnerability was detected in kLOsk adloop up to 0.9.0. This vulnerability affects the function _v
description: A vulnerability was detected in kLOsk adloop up to 0.9.0. This vulnerability affects the function _validate_urls of the file src/adloop/ads/write.py. Performing a manipulation of the argument final_url results in server-side request forgery. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. Upgrading to version 0.10.0 is able to resolve this issue. The patch is named 217399723e3a2fb39389e5355d49ed80aaf9ea7c. Upgrading the affected component is advised.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit a server-side request forgery (SSRF) flaw, potentially enabling unauthorized access to internal systems, data exfiltration, or further exploitation of the affected system. The exploit is public, increasing the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade kLOsk adloop to version 0.10.0 or later to apply the patch (217399723e3a2fb39389e5355d49ed80aaf9ea7c).
Conduct a security audit to identify any unauthorized access or exploitation attempts related to this vulnerability.
Implement monitoring and alerting mechanisms to detect unusual traffic patterns or SSRF attempts targeting the affected function.

title: CVE-2026-15524 - A security vulnerability has been detected in alioshr memory-bank-mcp up to 0.2.1/3.1. This affects
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in alioshr memory-bank-mcp up to 0.2.1/3.1. This affects an unknown part of the file list-project-files-validation-factory.ts. Such manipulation of the argument projectName leads to path traversal. Local access is required to approach this attack. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15524.

title: CVE-2026-15522 - A security flaw has been discovered in tugcantopaloglu godot-mcp 2.0.0. Affected by this vulnerabili
description: A security flaw has been discovered in tugcantopaloglu godot-mcp 2.0.0. Affected by this vulnerability is the function validatePath of the file build/index.js of the component run_project. The manipulation of the argument projectPath results in path traversal. Attacking locally is a requirement. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. Upgrading to version 3.0.0 addresses this issue. The patch is identified as eb63add552aa4bd9205395cf91b40654654a3cf2. It is suggested to upgrade the affected component.

description: A vulnerability was identified in makafeli n8n-workflow-builder up to 0.11.0. Affected is an unknown function of the file build/server.cjs of the component update_node_from_file. title: CVE-2026-15520 - A vulnerability was determined in GNU LibreDWG 0.13.4-154-g0b573035. This impacts the function decom
description: A vulnerability was determined in GNU LibreDWG 0.13.4-154-g0b573035. This impacts the function decompress_R2004_section of the file src/decode.c of the component R2004 Section Decompression. Executing a manipulation can lead to heap-based buffer overflow. The attack requires local access. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. Upgrading to version 0.14.8396 will fix this issue. This patch is called 3d0f9fc2eddbd6579c99af3111c37c98f03475d0. You should upgrade the affected component.

description: A security flaw has been discovered in Wavlink WL-NU516U1 260515. This affects the function wlink_uci_set_value of the file /cgi-bin/adm.cgi.
